deployment_question: How does the image model perform on scanners, sites, protocols, or contrasts absent from training?
definition: Hold out all images from specific scanner vendors, acquisition protocols, hospitals, image contrasts, or centers to test acquisition-domain transfer.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: sets same-acquisition-domain overlap to zero for held-out imaging domains

implementation_recipe: Select the acquisition domain to hold out.
Assign all images from held-out scanner/site/protocol to test.
Audit image intensity, protocol, and population differences.
quality_gates: Check whether labels or disease prevalence are site-confounded.
failure_modes: Scanner holdout can be confounded with patient population or disease prevalence.
Image preprocessing can leak test-domain statistics.
A model may learn site artifacts instead of pathology.

citations.title: The Impact of Scanner Domain Shift on Deep Learning Performance in Medical Imaging
citations.evidence: Experimentally evaluates scanner-domain shift and reports degraded deep learning performance.

citations.title: Machine Learning with Multi-Site Imaging Data
citations.evidence: Shows scanner differences create bias and can lead to overestimated performance.
